what_it_does: This file is an agent-plan noticeboard item that lists seven analytical lenses to apply to a design/code substrate and states how their outputs should be stored and read.
observations: Lists seven named lenses: iOS interaction designer, collaborative-annotation product researcher, knowledge-graph/systems architect, skeptic/adversary, accessibility & voice lens, radical minimalist, and a different-domain heavy annotator.
Each lens is assigned a focus area such as web research, real-device feel, failure modes, graph folding, prerequisites, voice-first path, or markup-for-a-living expectations.
Specifies that each agent's take becomes its own document in the switcher, not merged into one flattened answer.
Plans to surface tensions and convergences as a map rather than as a decision.
Uses noticeboard frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Links to parent item board://item-2e030ebc via part_of.
Created and updated timestamps are identical.
standouts: The anti-merge output policy: each perspective stays its own document, and tensions/convergences are mapped separately.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and Markdown body.
Uses a numbered list for the lenses.
References a parent item through a part_of link.
No code symbols or executable content.
concerns: Assumes a 'switcher' UI concept that is not defined in this file.
Does not state selection criteria or priority order for the lenses.
Does not define how the tensions/convergences map is produced or what format it takes.
Created and updated timestamps are the same, suggesting the item has not been revised.
notes: A planning note that proposes running seven distinct review lenses and keeping their outputs separate while mapping where they agree or conflict.
questions: What is the specific design or code substrate being reviewed?
What is the 'switcher' and how does a reader navigate these output documents?
How are the tensions and convergences map generated and represented?
What triggers each lens/agent to run?
What is the content and role of the parent item item-2e030ebc?
Are there criteria for adding, cutting, or changing lenses?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a plan for lenses and where their outputs land.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a structured, multi-agent review ritual that produces readable, separate perspective documents and a conflict/convergence map rather than a single recommendation.
novelty: The explicit policy against merging agent outputs into one flattened answer, opting instead for per-lens documents plus a separate tensions/convergences map.
contribution: This file contributes a reusable planning pattern for decomposing a design review into named analytical lenses and a policy for preserving divergent outputs as first-class documents with a tensions map.
summary_for_embedding: An agent-plan noticeboard item listing seven analytical lenses to apply to a design/code substrate and specifying that each lens produces its own document, with tensions and convergences surfaced as a separate map rather than merged into a single recommendation.
intention_for_embedding: To organize a multi-perspective review where diverse agent outputs remain separate and readable on mobile, with conflicts mapped instead of flattened.
